rulings.Case Title: CONSUELO LOPEZ et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 30, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT THE C.P. HALL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A hearing is required to address the verification to plaintiff's discovery responses and the appropriateness of granting the motion for summary judgment. The Paro Declaration is not admitted for lack of foundation. Plaintiff's evidence does not raise a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ANASTOS VS. BOB PECHOULTRES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Supplemental Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 30, 2008, line 9. PLAINTIFF CHARLES ANASTOS' Motion For Supplemental Complaint is continued to January 7, 2009. Both parties to provide courtesy copies of their papers to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL F. DIESSNER et al VS. THE SILVER FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 30, 2008, line 10. PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL DIESSNER AND ROBIN DIESSNER'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. Although plaintiff has not complied with California Rule of Court regarding specifying the additional allegations, plaintiff's motion is granted to the extent plaintiff seeks only to add parties. See Ryan G. v. Dept. of Transportation (1986) 180 Cal. App. 3d 1102. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: NATOMA COURT, LLC VS. KOI KWAN YUEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Deft'S Demurrer To Pltf'S Complaint-Unlawful Detainer; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Of Deft'S Demurrer.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 30, 2008, line 17. DEFENDANT KOI YUEN'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Complaint is overruled, 5 days to answer. Shulman v. Vera (1980) 108 Cal App 3d 552 supports the inclusion of other charges in the 3 day notice. Cal-America Income Property Fund IV v. Ito (1984) 161 Cal App 3d 583, 586 allows plaintiff to claim property taxes and utilities beyond 1 year under CCP section 1161. =(302/PJM/VC)
